frameworks.name: optimal filtering
frameworks.one_line: probabilistic filter that separates signal from non-salient repetitive input

frameworks.name: attentional filter account of habituation
frameworks.one_line: formalizes the long-standing filter metaphor into an explicit probabilistic model

core_question: Can habituation to repetitive stimuli be explained as the operation of an optimal probabilistic filter?
key_assumptions: responding tracks an inferred signal rather than raw input
non-salient repetitive stimuli are statistically distinguishable from salient ones
an observer optimally filters input under a simple probabilistic model
manipulations: stimulus repetition / salience structure within the probabilistic model
key_findings: the optimal-filter model reproduces the major characteristics of habituation
it also captures relatively neglected characteristics of habituation
interpretations: habituation can be understood as a form of optimal filtering rather than passive fatigue
